thoughtHistory.thought: Mapping all areas to audit for 100% correctness in Hawkeye-Sterling. The tool is an AML compliance platform. From session history, known pending items: (1) STC academic search - never built, (2) saved-searches withGuard auth - marked as 'worth confirming', (3) newsAdapters Tavily/Exa/Perplexity - just fixed TS errors, need to verify wiring in the aggregator, (4) GDELT status fix - committed, need to verify it reads correctly, (5) STR taxonomy 20 groups 2012 items - committed and clean. NEW items discovered in last pass: (6) unsafe content[0] casts - fixed in 7 routes, (7) missing fetch timeouts in deep-research - fixed, (8) missing gate.headers in country-risk - fixed. What remains unknown: are there other API routes with broken LLM wiring? Are frontend components actually calling the right endpoints? Are there any routes that import functions but never call them? Is the intelligence brain (super-brain, weaponized-brain, etc) properly wired? Are there 3s SLA violations beyond what was fixed?

Audit plan — 8 parallel tracks: TRACK A: TypeScript clean build (already confirmed 0 errors). TRACK B: Wiring audit — do the 3 new adapters (Tavily, Exa, Perplexity) appear in getNewsAdapters() aggregator? TRACK C: STC academic search — was this ever supposed to be built or just discussed? Check if there's a typology-library or similar route that needs it. TRACK D: Routes with HARD_DEADLINE_MS / 3s SLA — find all routes that do LLM calls without a timeout guard. TRACK E: Routes missing withGuard — compare exported POST/GET handlers against those using withGuard. TRACK F: Silent errors in non-API code (intelligence layer, cache, etc). TRACK G: Frontend wiring — do key components reference endpoints that exist? TRACK H: Status page accuracy — GDELT fix, intel-status endpoint listing all adapters. Execute all tracks in parallel.
